paper_title: Triangle Ramsey numbers of complete graphs
summary: Conjecture 1.2 posits that for all $s \leq t$, $r_{K_s}(K_t) = \binom{r(K_t)}{s}$. The case $s = 2$ is classical (Chv\u00e1tal). The source paper itself resolves $s = 3$ for all sufficiently large $t$ as its main result (Theorem 1.3), via a chromatic-graph lemma: every $r$-chromatic graph in which every edge lies in at least $K$ triangles contains at least $\binom{r}{3}$ triangles. No subsequent work extending the result to $s \geq 4$ was found in the indexed literature as of May 2026.

notes: The conjecture is partially settled in the source paper itself ($s = 3$ proved for all sufficiently large $t$). The general case ($s \geq 4$) remains open. The paper was published in Journal of Combinatorial Theory, Series B, vol. 176 (2026), pp. 268–286. No follow-up paper resolving additional cases was found.
